18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
45		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
46		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid a poll-interval-sized delay
47		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
48		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
49		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
50		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
51	}

53	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
54	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
55	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
56	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
57	
58	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
59	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
60	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
61	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
62	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
63	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
64	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
65	
66	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
67	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
68	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
69	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
70	// advance the base branch.
71	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
72	
73	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
74	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
75	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
76	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
77	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
78	// worker.
79	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
80	
81	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
82	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
83	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
84	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
85	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
86	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
87	//
88	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
89	// the cancellation immediately.
90	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
91		if ctx.Err() != nil {
92			return false
93		}
94		if runtime.Log != nil {
95			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
96		}
97		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
98			Kind:      "loop-error",
99			Summary:   operation + " failed",
100			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
101			Actor:     assignee,
102			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
103			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
104		})
105		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
106		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
107		result.Failures++
108		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
109		return true
110	}
111	
112	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
113	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
114	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
115	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
116		if d <= 0 {
117			return 0
118		}
119		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
120			return MaxLoopCooldown
121		}
122		return d
123	}

13	// TestZeroConfigRetryEscalationPolicy verifies the retry/escalation policy
14	// composes correctly when the loop starts from the new metadata-driven
15	// "profile=cheap by default" path (commit 3ddf3d52, ddx-b2c9a245). It is a
16	// follow-up to AC6 of ddx-b790449b: confirm that transient failures retry
17	// within the cheap tier (no escalation) and substantive failures escalate
18	// cheap -> standard -> smart.
19	//
20	// The test drives a simulated tier ladder using the real escalation
21	// primitives (InferTier, IsInfrastructureFailure, ShouldEscalate,
22	// TierToProfile, BuildEscalationSummary). It does not require a running
23	// agent service or harness binary.
24	func TestZeroConfigRetryEscalationPolicy(t *testing.T) {
25		// 1. Unflagged bead: no tier label, no kind, short description.
26		// InferTier must default to cheap so the loop starts at the cheap
27		// tier instead of the previous always-cheap fallback or the SD-024
28		// "no profile" path.
29		b := &bead.Bead{
30			ID:          "ddx-zero-config-001",
31			Title:       "trivial cleanup, unflagged",
32			Description: "minor doc tweak",
33		}
34		startTier := escalation.InferTier(b)
35		require.Equal(t, escalation.TierCheap, startTier,
36			"unflagged bead must infer cheap tier (zero-config default)")
37		require.Equal(t, "cheap", escalation.TierToProfile(startTier),
38			"cheap tier maps to 'cheap' profile string consumed by LoadAndResolve")
39	
40		// 2. Transient infrastructure failure on cheap tier (e.g. 502 from
41		// the inference host). The policy is: do NOT escalate; defer with a
42		// retry-after and try the same tier again. The model wasn't given
43		// a fair chance, so escalation budget must not be consumed.
44		transientStatus := ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
45		transientDetail := "provider 502 Bad Gateway: connection refused"
46		require.True(t,
47			escalation.IsInfrastructureFailure(transientStatus, transientDetail),
48			"502 Bad Gateway on execution_failed must be classified as infrastructure failure")
49		require.True(t,
50			escalation.ShouldEscalate(transientStatus),
51			"execution_failed itself remains escalatable; the loop must consult IsInfrastructureFailure first to defer-vs-escalate")
52	
53		// Loop ordering invariant: when IsInfrastructureFailure is true the
54		// loop body defers (retry-after) instead of advancing the tier. The
55		// tier on the next attempt is therefore unchanged.
56		tierAfterTransient := startTier
57		require.Equal(t, escalation.TierCheap, tierAfterTransient,
58			"transient failure must not advance the tier ladder")
59	
60		// 3. Substantive failure on cheap tier (e.g. tests failed). Policy:
61		// escalate to the next tier (standard).
62		substantiveStatus := ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
63		substantiveDetail := "TestFoo failed: assertion mismatch"
64		require.False(t,
65			escalation.IsInfrastructureFailure(substantiveStatus, substantiveDetail),
66			"a model-capability failure detail must not match infrastructure patterns")
67		require.True(t,
68			escalation.ShouldEscalate(substantiveStatus),
69			"substantive execution_failed must escalate")
70		tierAfterCheapSubstantive := nextTier(tierAfterTransient)
71		require.Equal(t, escalation.TierStandard, tierAfterCheapSubstantive,
72			"cheap -> standard escalation step")
73	
74		// 4. Substantive failure on standard tier. Policy: escalate to smart.
75		standardStatus := ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
76		require.True(t,
77			escalation.ShouldEscalate(standardStatus),
78			"post_run_check_failed escalates")
79		tierAfterStandardSubstantive := nextTier(tierAfterCheapSubstantive)
80		require.Equal(t, escalation.TierSmart, tierAfterStandardSubstantive,
81			"standard -> smart escalation step")
82	
83		// 5. Smart tier succeeds. Build the escalation summary and verify
84		// the full trail is recorded with winning_tier=smart and the cheap
85		// transient attempt counted as wasted-but-not-escalating-budget.
86		attempts := []escalation.TierAttemptRecord{
87			{Tier: string(escalation.TierCheap), Harness: "agent", Model: "cheap-model", Status: transientStatus, CostUSD: 0.0, DurationMS: 800},
88			{Tier: string(escalation.TierCheap), Harness: "agent", Model: "cheap-model", Status: substantiveStatus, CostUSD: 0.02, DurationMS: 1500},
89			{Tier: string(escalation.TierStandard), Harness: "codex", Model: "standard-model", Status: standardStatus, CostUSD: 0.15, DurationMS: 3400},
90			{Tier: string(escalation.TierSmart), Harness: "claude", Model: "smart-model", Status: escalation.SuccessStatus, CostUSD: 0.80, DurationMS: 9000},
91		}
92		summary := escalation.BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, string(escalation.TierSmart))
93		require.Equal(t, string(escalation.TierSmart), summary.WinningTier,
94			"smart tier won the escalation")
95		require.Len(t, summary.TiersAttempted, 4,
96			"trail records all attempts including the deferred-transient cheap attempt")
97		assert.InDelta(t, 0.97, summary.TotalCostUSD, 1e-9)
98		assert.InDelta(t, 0.17, summary.WastedCostUSD, 1e-9,
99			"cheap-substantive (0.02) + standard (0.15) wasted; cheap-transient billed $0; smart succeeded")
100		assert.Equal(t, string(escalation.TierCheap), summary.TiersAttempted[0].Tier,
101			"first attempt was cheap (the new metadata-driven default)")
102		assert.Equal(t, string(escalation.TierSmart), summary.TiersAttempted[3].Tier,
103			"final attempt was smart (top of ladder)")
104	}

106	// TestZeroConfigEscalationSummaryEventBody verifies the escalation summary
107	// is emitted as a kind:escalation-summary bead event when the cheap-default
108	// ladder runs to completion. This is the operator-visible artifact of the
109	// retry/escalation policy.
110	func TestZeroConfigEscalationSummaryEventBody(t *testing.T) {
111		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
112		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
113		target := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-zero-config-002", Title: "cheap-default ladder", Priority: 0}
114		require.NoError(t, store.Create(target))
115	
116		attempts := []escalation.TierAttemptRecord{
117			{Tier: string(escalation.TierCheap), Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, CostUSD: 0.01, DurationMS: 1000},
118			{Tier: string(escalation.TierStandard), Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, CostUSD: 0.10, DurationMS: 2000},
119			{Tier: string(escalation.TierSmart), Status: escalation.SuccessStatus, CostUSD: 0.50, DurationMS: 4000},
120		}
121		require.NoError(t,
122			escalation.AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(
123				store, target.ID, "test-worker", attempts, string(escalation.TierSmart), time.Unix(1, 0).UTC()))
124	
125		events, err := store.Events(target.ID)
126		require.NoError(t, err)
127		var found *bead.BeadEvent
128		for i := range events {
129			if events[i].Kind == "escalation-summary" {
130				found = &events[i]
131				break
132			}
133		}
134		require.NotNil(t, found, "escalation-summary event must be appended for the cheap-default ladder run")
135		assert.Contains(t, found.Summary, "winning_tier=smart")
136		assert.Contains(t, found.Summary, "attempts=3")
137		assert.Contains(t, found.Body, `"tier":"cheap"`,
138			"summary body records cheap as the first-attempted tier (zero-config default)")
139		assert.Contains(t, found.Body, `"tier":"standard"`)
140		assert.Contains(t, found.Body, `"tier":"smart"`)
141	}

143	// nextTier advances one rung up the cheap -> standard -> smart ladder.
144	// Mirrors the implicit ladder used by the execute-loop when escalating
145	// beyond a substantive failure. Smart is the top; further calls stay
146	// at smart (callers exhaust escalation when ShouldEscalate fires at smart).
147	func nextTier(t escalation.ModelTier) escalation.ModelTier {
148		switch t {
149		case escalation.TierCheap:
150			return escalation.TierStandard
151		case escalation.TierStandard:
152			return escalation.TierSmart
153		default:
154			return escalation.TierSmart
155		}
156	}

385	const (
386		DefaultHarness = "codex"
387		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
388		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
389		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
390		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
391		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
392		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
393		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
394		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
395		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
396		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
397		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
398		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
399		DefaultLogDir      = ".ddx/agent-logs"
400	)

402	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
403	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
404	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
405	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
406	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
407		if configured == "" {
408			configured = DefaultLogDir
409		}
410		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
411			return configured
412		}
413		if projectRoot == "" {
414			return configured
415		}
416		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
417	}

270	func (r *RealGitOps) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
271		return r.ResolveRev(dir, "HEAD")
272	}

274	func (r *RealGitOps) ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error) {
275		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", rev).Output()
276		if err != nil {
277			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %w", rev, err)
278		}
279		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
280	}

282	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error {
283		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "add", "--detach", wtPath, rev).CombinedOutput()
284		if err != nil {
285			return fmt.Errorf("git worktree add: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
286		}
287		return nil
288	}
289	
290	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error {
291		_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "remove", "--force", wtPath).Run()
292		return nil
293	}
294	
295	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error) {
296		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "list", "--porcelain").Output()
297		if err != nil {
298			return nil, fmt.Errorf("git worktree list: %w", err)
299		}
300		var paths []string
301		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
302			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "worktree ") {
303				paths = append(paths, strings.TrimPrefix(line, "worktree "))
304			}
305		}
306		return paths, nil
307	}
308	
309	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreePrune(dir string) error {
310		return internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "prune").Run()
311	}

313	// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha via `git update-ref`.
314	func (r *RealGitOps) UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error {
315		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", ref, sha).CombinedOutput()
316		if err != nil {
317			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
318		}
319		return nil

3	// agent.DefaultBeadReviewer pipeline and returns a try.Outcome via the C1
4	// ToOutcome adapter. It has no production callers at end of C2; the

18	// AttemptOpts bundles the knobs Attempt needs to drive the legacy
19	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig + LandBeadResult + DefaultBeadReviewer pipeline.
20	//
21	// Tests inject Executor to supply a canned ExecuteBeadReport without
22	// touching git or a real harness. When Executor is non-nil it short-circuits
23	// the legacy pipeline entirely; the remaining fields are ignored.
24	type AttemptOpts struct {
25		// Executor is an optional injection seam. When non-nil, Attempt delegates
26		// to it instead of calling ExecuteBeadWithConfig + LandBeadResult.
27		Executor agent.ExecuteBeadExecutor
28	
29		// ProjectRoot is the repo the bead executes in.
30		ProjectRoot string
31

36		Runtime agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime

39		GitOps agent.GitOps
40	
41		// OrchestratorGitOps is the parent-side git surface (UpdateRef).
42		OrchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps
43	
44		// LandingOpts controls how LandBeadResult lands a completed worker result.
45		LandingOpts agent.BeadLandingOptions
46	
47		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is invoked after a successful merge to validate
48		// the commit against the bead's AC. When nil, post-merge review is skipped.
49		Reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
50	}

52	// Attempt runs one bead attempt and returns a try.Outcome.
53	//
54	// When opts.Executor is non-nil, Attempt delegates to it and converts the
55	// returned ExecuteBeadReport via ToOutcome — the path covered by
56	// TestAttempt_WrapsLegacyExecutor.
57	//
58	// Otherwise Attempt drives the legacy pipeline:
59	//
60	//  1. agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig — run the agent in an isolated worktree.
61	//  2. agent.LandBeadResult — merge or preserve, evaluate gates.
62	//  3. opts.Reviewer.ReviewBead (best effort) — optional post-merge review.
63	//
64	// The store argument is reserved for the C3+ migration where Attempt will
65	// own bead-tracker bookkeeping (claim, heartbeat, close). At C2 it is
66	// accepted but not yet used; the legacy loop still owns those calls.
67	func Attempt(ctx context.Context, store agent.ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID string, opts AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error) {
68		_ = store // reserved for C3+ migration; legacy loop still owns store I/O.
69	
70		if opts.Executor != nil {
71			report, err := opts.Executor.Execute(ctx, beadID)
72			if err != nil {
73				out := ToOutcome(report)
74				if out.BeadID == "" {
75					out.BeadID = beadID
76				}
77				return out, err
78			}
79			out := ToOutcome(report)
80			if out.BeadID == "" {
81				out.BeadID = beadID
82			}
83			return out, nil
84		}
85	
86		res, execErr := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, opts.ProjectRoot, beadID, opts.Rcfg, opts.Runtime, opts.GitOps)
87		if execErr != nil && res == nil {
88			return Outcome{BeadID: beadID}, execErr
89		}
90	
91		if res != nil {
92			landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(opts.ProjectRoot, res, opts.OrchestratorGitOps, opts.LandingOpts)
93			if landErr == nil {
94				agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
95			} else if execErr == nil {
96				execErr = landErr
97			}
98		}
99	
100		if opts.Reviewer != nil && res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
101			// C3 (ddx-a921ff01): drive the full post-merge review state machine
102			// — verdict events, close-on-APPROVE, reopen-on-REQUEST_CHANGES /
103			// BLOCK, triage callout — via the extracted RunPostMergeReview that
104			// replaces the 180-line inline review block in execute_bead_loop.go.
105			// Store errors are swallowed at this layer because the legacy loop
106			// (which currently owns claim/heartbeat/close bookkeeping) handles
107			// them via handleOutcomeStoreError; the C3 shell predates that
108			// migration (C7) and so cannot route the same way.
109			report := agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
110				BeadID:      beadID,
111				AttemptID:   res.AttemptID,
112				Harness:     res.Harness,
113				Provider:    res.Provider,
114				Model:       res.Model,
115				ActualPower: res.ActualPower,
116				SessionID:   res.SessionID,
117				BaseRev:     res.BaseRev,
118				ResultRev:   res.ResultRev,
119			}
120			_ = agent.RunPostMergeReview(ctx, agent.PostMergeReviewInput{
121				Bead:        bead.Bead{ID: beadID},
122				Report:      report,
123				Reviewer:    opts.Reviewer,
124				Store:       store,
125				ProjectRoot: opts.ProjectRoot,
126				Rcfg:        opts.Rcfg,
127				Assignee:    opts.Rcfg.Assignee(),
128				Now:         time.Now,
129			})
130		}
131	
132		report := reportFromResult(res)
133		out := ToOutcome(report)
134		if out.BeadID == "" {
135			out.BeadID = beadID
136		}
137		return out, execErr
138	}

141	// ToOutcome consumes. It is intentionally narrow — the loop's full report
142	// synthesis (tier, profile telemetry, escalation count) is not needed for
143	// the C2 shell and gets built out as callers migrate in C3+.
144	func reportFromResult(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) agent.ExecuteBeadReport {
145		if res == nil {
146			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}
147		}
148		return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
149			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
150			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
151			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
152			Harness:            res.Harness,
153			Provider:           res.Provider,
154			Model:              res.Model,

156			Status:             res.Status,

159			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
160			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
161			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
162			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
163			CostUSD:            res.CostUSD,
164			DurationMS:         int64(res.DurationMS),
165		}
166	}
